metadata.seed: So work's a little bit of a different story. We have our daily meetings, and. I. Actually had to pull, like, one of my coworkers aside because I feel like they undermine me all the time, and they, like, they don't give me the respect, basically, that I deserve. So I took her outside after the meeting and leaned into her about it. And afterwards, I mean, the next day, because we have these meetings every day, she, like, she came back and came at me again, but it was worse even after talking to her.

patient.query: So work's a little bit of a different story. We have our daily meetings, and. I. Actually had to pull, like, one of my coworkers aside because I feel like they undermine me all the time, and they, like, they don't give me the respect, basically, that I deserve. So I took her outside after the meeting and leaned into her about it. And afterwards, I mean, the next day, because we have these meetings every day, she, like, she came back and came at me again, but it was worse even after talking to her.

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re feeling pretty frustrated and disappointed after the conversation. What do you think might have led her to respond the way she did? How might you view this situation from a different perspective?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess so. I feel like I put a lot into that conversation, and it just... fell flat. It’s frustrating because I really thought she'd see my potential, you know? But then, maybe she just doesn’t understand me or my work like I thought she did. I can’t help but feel kind of small after it.

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re feeling disappointed that the conversation didn’t go as you hoped. What evidence do you have that she didn’t see your potential? Could there be other ways she might have responded that you’re not seeing right now?

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I suppose I just felt like there was no enthusiasm or support from her side. It’s hard not to take that personally. I guess I’ve been feeling like it’s a pattern, like I keep putting myself out there but don't get much back. Maybe I’m just focusing on the negative? But it still stings, you know?

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re noticing a pattern that’s been painful. What are some moments when you did put yourself out there and did receive support or a positive response? How might you explore other explanations for the lack of enthusiasm you felt?

patient.query: I mean, there have been a few times when I've had great feedback—like that project I led last year. My team was really behind me, and it felt amazing. But then, with this recent conversation, it feels like I'm just getting knocked back down. I guess it’s hard for me to think of other reasons for her reaction. I just keep wondering if I’m the problem. Maybe I’m not trying hard enough? It’s just... frustrating.

automatic_thoughts: Maybe I'm not trying hard enough

neutral: Tends to be sensitive to criticism (finding)
Tends to appear indifferent to praise or criticism (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re feeling like the conversation was a personal failure. What evidence do you have that this reflects your worth? Could there be other reasons for her reaction that you haven’t considered yet?

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: anxiety about saying the wrong thing
retrieved_concepts_used.label: neutral

patient.query: I just feel really frustrated. There have been moments when I’ve done well, like that project I led last year when my team was super supportive. But after this recent conversation, it’s like I’m back to feeling small and unsure of myself. I can’t shake the feeling that maybe I’m the problem. It just stings so much, and I keep wondering if I'm not trying hard enough.

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re feeling frustrated and small after that conversation, and it’s understandable to feel upset when you think you might be the problem. It can be hard to see your own strengths when you’re in that place. What evidence do you have that you might not be the problem, or that you’re doing enough?

patient.query: I guess it’s just hard to feel that way right now. Those moments when I led that project felt great, but they seem so far in the past now. It's like I keep replaying the negative feedback, and that overshadows the good stuff. I don’t know, maybe I’m just stuck in this loop of doubt. It feels like I can’t see the positive things clearly anymore.

triggers: moments when I led that project
automatic_thoughts: I keep replaying the negative feedback
I’m just stuck in this loop of doubt
I can’t see the positive things clearly anymore

llm_response.response: I hear how heavy that replaying of the negative feedback feels, and it’s understandable that the bright moments of leading the project seem distant right now. It can be exhausting to stay stuck in that loop of doubt, especially when it feels like the positives are slipping away. You’re not alone in feeling this way, and it’s okay to acknowledge that it’s hard to see the good side at the moment.

patient.query: Thanks for saying that. It really does feel exhausting, like I’m just spinning my wheels and not getting anywhere. I keep trying to remind myself of those bright moments, but they don’t feel as real when I'm in this doubt. It's like I keep thinking, "What if I’m just fooling myself?” I just wish I could shake this feeling and see things more clearly again.

automatic_thoughts: What if I’m just fooling myself?
behaviors: trying to remind myself of bright moments

entailment: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy sense of fatigue and uncertainty, feeling like you’re stuck in a loop that’s hard to break. Those bright moments you try to recall can feel distant when doubt clouds your perspective. It’s understandable to want to shake off that heaviness and find clearer footing again.

patient.query: Yeah, that’s exactly it. It’s like I want to hold onto those good memories, but they feel like they’re getting overshadowed by all this doubt and fatigue. I just wish I could have more of those bright moments, you know? It's frustrating feeling like I'm not at my best, and it's hard to see a way out of this.

automatic_thoughts: I wish I could have more bright moments
It's hard to see a way out

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re feeling stuck and exhausted, and it’s hard to hold onto those bright moments when doubt and fatigue creep in. That frustration is understandable, especially when it feels like you’re not at your best. It can be tough to see a way forward when everything feels overwhelming.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is tough. I keep waiting for that spark of motivation to come back, but it just seems so far away. And I think about how much I want to do well, but then I get caught up in that doubt and it stops me in my tracks. It feels like I’m just going in circles, and I don’t know how to break out of it.

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re feeling stuck and frustrated, and that can be exhausting. What small step might feel doable right now that could help you move a bit forward?

patient.query: It really is tough. I keep hoping that spark of motivation will come back, but it feels so distant. I want to do well, but that doubt just holds me back. It's like I'm stuck in this loop, going in circles, and I can't find a way out.

automatic_thoughts: hoping that spark of motivation will come back

neutral: Loss of interest in previously enjoyable activity (finding)

llm_response.response:
I hear how frustrated and stuck you feel right now, and it’s understandable that the doubt feels like a heavy barrier.  
What evidence do you have that the spark of motivation is truly out of reach, and are there any moments, however small, when you’ve felt a hint of it?  
Could you think of one tiny action that might help you break the loop and start feeling a bit more in control?
